channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: First local bird flu case confirmed in South Australia, wildlife at risk
item.description: Australian authorities have confirmed the first case of H5N1 bird flu in a local bird species, raising concerns about the virus's spread to wildlife. A juvenile New Zealand fur seal in New South Wales...

item.title: Manila road closures announced for July 10 anti-corruption rally
item.description: The Manila Police District has announced temporary road closures around Liwasang Bonifacio, Mendiola, and Ayala Bridge on July 10 for an 'anti-corruption' rally led by the United People's Initiative (...

item.title: USA to Share 2026 World Cup Prize Money with Women's Team
item.description: The USA men's football team will share their 2026 FIFA World Cup prize money with the women's national team, following a collective bargaining agreement (CBA) signed in 2022. The deal, which came afte...

item.title: Over 1,000 Nigerians Evacuated from South Africa as Fifth Batch Arrives in Lagos
item.description: The Federal Government's evacuation of Nigerians from South Africa has reached a new milestone as the fifth batch of returnees arrived in Lagos on Thursday evening. The Air Peace flight, which brought...

item.title: National Treasury Withholds Funding from KZN Municipalities Over Governance Failures
item.description: The National Treasury has temporarily withheld equitable share transfers from seven KwaZulu-Natal municipalities due to governance failures, sparking concerns about the impact on vulnerable communitie...

item.title: Tanzania invests in traditional medicine research to bridge healthcare gap
item.description: The Tanzanian government has announced plans to bring together experts from various fields to conduct comprehensive research on traditional and herbal medicines. This initiative aims to integrate indi...

item.title: Trump's alleged Iran strike aimed to dominate NATO summit attention, biographer claims
item.description: A Trump biographer suggests the president's recent strike on Iran was a deliberate move to dominate attention at the NATO summit, according to a podcast interview. Michael Wolff, author of 'Fire and F...

item.title: Brush Fire Near Lake Piru Sparks Evacuations and Firefighting Efforts
item.description: A brush fire ignited near Lake Piru on Thursday afternoon, prompting evacuation warnings in the area. The blaze, known as the Grande fire, started at 2:20 p.m. south of the 126 Freeway, east of Lake P...

item.title: Heavy Rains Lead to School Closures in Delhi NCR
item.description: Heavy rainfall and thunderstorms continue across Delhi-NCR on July 10, 2026, prompting the IMD to issue orange and yellow alerts. Schools in Ghaziabad have been ordered closed due to extreme rain, wit...

item.title: Getdown Services Release New Single 'Cha Cha Slide' Exploring Themes of Social Anxiety and Connection
item.description: Getdown Services, the Bristol-based disco duo, have released their latest single 'Cha Cha Slide', described by vocalist Josh Law as 'the saddest song we’ve ever written'. The track, part of their upco...

item.title: Upcoming Events in Cebu: ITZY, BINI Concerts and 'On Your Feet' Show
item.description: The weekend schedule features exciting events for music lovers and entertainment enthusiasts. K-pop groups ITZY and BINI will be performing live in Cebu, offering fans a chance to experience their ene...

item.title: McDonald's Canberra: From Casual Jobs to Lifelong Careers Celebrates 50 Years
item.description: The article highlights how McDonald's Canberra has transformed casual teenage jobs into lifelong careers over five decades. Ben Stockbridge, who started at 15, now owns seven restaurants in the ACT, w...

item.title: PM Modi visits Melbourne Cricket Ground during Australia tour, highlights cricket ties and stronger India-Australia partnership
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, accompanied by Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese, visited the iconic Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG) during his official visit to Australia. At the stadium, PM Mo...

item.title: APC Government Allegedly Aiming to Keep El-Rufai in Custody Until 2027 Elections
item.description: The African Democratic Party (ADC) has accused the ruling All Progressives Congress (APC) of holding former Kaduna State Governor Nasir El-Rufai in custody to suppress opposition ahead of the 2027 ele...

item.title: DA Condemns Gang Violence in Cape Flats, Calls for Effective Policing Strategies
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has condemned recent fatal shootings in Tafelsig and Philippi East, where seven people were killed and five injured, stating that military deployments alone cannot curb ga...

item.title: Vatican Ambassador Brian Burch Calls for Apology After Leak Allegations
item.description: María Teresita Armstrong-Matta, the Vatican ambassador appointed by President Donald Trump, has publicly demanded an apology and assistance in identifying the source of a leak that allegedly portrayed...

item.title: Former Lawyer Sentenced for R40m Theft and Money Laundering
item.description: An 82-year-old former attorney, Solomon Slom, has been sentenced to 10 years in prison for embezzling nearly R40m from an escrow account. The Johannesburg specialised commercial crimes court convicted...

item.title: CNN Pundit Mocks Commentator Over Lack of Knowledge About Iran Conflict Duration
item.description: Conservative CNN pundit Scott Jennings mocked former Trump White House aide Caroline Sunshine during a debate over U.S. military strategy in Iran. The exchange began when Sunshine admitted she was una...

item.title: Canada and Saudi Arabia Strengthen Trade Ties After Diplomatic Reconciliation
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney's historic visit to Saudi Arabia marks a significant shift in bilateral relations, with both nations focusing on economic cooperation rather than past tensions. During his t...

item.title: Vice President Duterte Urges Officials to Prioritize National Progress Over Political Interests
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte criticized politicians who advocate for transparency and accountability but prioritize their own political interests, urging them instead to focus on improving Filipinos' l...
